channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 30 days.

item.title: FOMAS Group Special Prize 2026 (within Arte Laguna Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: FOMAS Group (in collaboration with Arte Laguna Prize)

Location: Italy (FOMAS HQ Lecco; presented within Arte Laguna at Arsenale Nord, Venice)

Award: First prize EUR 7,000; second EUR 4,000; third EUR 3,000 (all taxes included). NOTE: works are entered via the Arte Laguna Prize, which has a registration fee (approx. EUR 97-122). The winning works become FOMAS Group property (rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation transferred to FOMAS).

Duration: One-off special prize awarded within Arte Laguna Prize 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Special Prize from FOMAS Group, an Italian multinational in forging/rolling/metal powders, awarded within the Arte Laguna Prize 2026. THEME: 'the journey of steel in its crossing from hot to cold' - a metamorphosis of matter from incandescent casting to a cooled, smooth, precise surface; works should evoke this transition. FORMAT: hangable panel or canvas, dimensions between 1.5x1.5 m (minimum) and 3x2 m (maximum); unconventional materials encouraged. THREE AWARDS to nominated works: EUR 7,000 first, EUR 4,000 second, EUR 3,000 third (taxes included), paid after the work is presented. IMPORTANT CONDITION: the winning works become the property of FOMAS Group, with the artists ceding ownership and rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation for institutional and promotional purposes. APPLY by submitting via the Arte Laguna Prize portal (which has a registration fee, approx. EUR 97-122 for the full prize). Registration for the FOMAS Special Award closes 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Reply AI Film Festival 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Reply

Location: Online submissions; premiere event in Venice, Italy

Award: First prize EUR 8,000, second prize EUR 5,000, third prize EUR 2,000 (total prize pool EUR 30,000+), plus travel and 2-night Venice accommodation for finalists. No submission fee.

Duration: Annual short-film prize

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Annual short-film festival from Reply for films generated or enhanced through AI. Finalists are flown to a premiere event in Venice and compete for a prize pool of around EUR 30,000+ (1st EUR 8,000, 2nd EUR 5,000, 3rd EUR 2,000, plus additional category awards). ELIGIBILITY: open internationally; submissions must be generated or enhanced through AI. No submission fee. Travel and a 2-night Venice stay covered for selected finalists. Deadline 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Malakta x Kulturhuset Dux: Community Art Commission and Residency 2026 (Kristinestad)
item.description:
Organisation: Art in Malakta rf (with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency)

Location: Kristinestad / Malax, Finland

Award: EUR 6,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 materials budget, with travel and residency accommodation included. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency 1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026 (Phase 1) plus production phase, with artwork completed by summer 2027 (Phase 2)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Commission and residency from Art in Malakta rf with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency to create a public artwork for Kulturhuset Dux or its immediate surroundings, in dialogue with Kristinestad and its residents. The medium is open (installation, object, sound, textile, light, digital); what matters is the relationship between the work, the site and the people. The project is rooted in Kristinestad's identity as a Cittaslow town and as Baltic Sea Cultural Pearl 2026. PHASE 1 (1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026): a one-month residency at Kristinestad Artist Residency for research, meetings, workshops, and artistic development. PHASE 2: production after the residency (remotely or at Malakta if needed); the artwork must be completed by summer 2027 and requires on-site presence. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists or artist collectives wanting to work site-specifically and in dialogue with residents. APPLY by email to communityart@malakta.fi by 31 May 2026 with a motivation letter, portfolio, CV and website link as a single PDF (max 5 MB). Interviews before 19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Knight-Georgetown Institute: Research Analyst 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Knight-Georgetown Institute (KGI), Georgetown University

Location: Washington, D.C., United States (hybrid; must be based in D.C.)

Award: Paid role: annual salary $60,000-$70,000. No application fee.

Duration: Full-time position

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Knight-Georgetown Institute (Georgetown's technology-policy institute) is hiring a Research Analyst to join its team at the intersection of technology, social science, law and public policy. The role involves synthesising academic research across disciplines, contributing to original research projects, supporting policy communications for state, federal and international audiences, and helping organise convenings with leading experts. WHAT THEY LOOK FOR: a Bachelor's in any field with relevant tech and policy coursework; 2+ years in research, data analysis or a related area; strong research-synthesis and written-communication skills; genuine interest in platform governance, algorithmic systems or AI policy; based in (or willing to relocate to) Washington, D.C. Bonus points for a graduate degree, experience with R/Python/Stata, or qualitative-research methods. Paid: annual salary $60,000-$70,000. Hybrid position; the posting does not state a citizenship or work-authorization requirement. NOTE: this is a job, not a grant or fellowship.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SOFTER: Cyberdeck Residency 2026 (Copenhagen)
item.description:
Organisation: SOFTER

Location: SOFTER Lab, Copenhagen, Denmark

Award: Per person and incl. VAT: EUR 2,500 participation fee, plus EUR 1,000 materials budget, EUR 100 lunch, and EUR 1,250 travel and accommodation support (for participants not based in Copenhagen). No application fee.

Duration: Two-week residency, 3-16 August 2026; tutorial deliverable by 1 September 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Two-week residency at SOFTER Lab in Copenhagen inviting creative technologists and curious makers to design, build and demystify cyberdecks (custom-built portable computers typically based on single-board computers like a Raspberry Pi, with a homemade case reflecting the builder's aesthetic). The residency frames cyberdecks as a way to reclaim agency and creativity within tech, bridging the DIY hacker community and creative technologists. Selected residents source materials (with a materials budget and pointers to local electronic recycling), build their own cyberdeck, document the process, and produce a beginner-friendly 10-20 minute video tutorial (material list, tools, any 3D-printing files) ready for publication on 1 September 2026. An optional show-and-tell workshop or talk can be hosted at SOFTER Lab or the SOFTER Digital Futures conference (1-3 October 2026). Facilities include a workspace, GoPro for documentation, 3D printer and filament, basic soldering, and design/graphics support, with three SOFTER check-ins (kick-off, mid-process, evaluation). WHO: a team of two (apply as a pair on one project, or two individuals working alongside each other); backgrounds from curious beginner to hardware expert; interest in hardware/teaching/documentation. Applicants are selected on idea originality, functionality, purpose and design. Deadline 1 June 2026, 12:00 CEST; decisions within two weeks. Contact: ida@softer.global.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: transmediale 2027: Lattice Labs Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: transmediale

Location: Berlin, Germany (in-person sprint and festival) plus online sessions

Award: Not paid (no artist fee or stipend). transmediale covers travel costs to Berlin (train prioritised; otherwise round-trip economy flights) for each participant to attend the festival and the design sprint, plus accommodation for the duration of both. Letters of invitation provided for visa applications. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-block programme: online vocabulary sessions, a 3-day Berlin design sprint (October 2026), online implementation/festival prep (Nov-Dec 2026), and presentation at transmediale festival (January 2027)

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

transmediale 2027's Lattice Labs are experimental working groups dedicated to rethinking and testing alternatives to technological monocultures of extraction and surveillance, in preparation of the festival. Each Lab has roughly five participants guided by an experienced artist or technologist, structured around flat hierarchies and peer-to-peer learning, with the aim of producing prototypes, tools and projects that articulate shared languages to organise, resist and live otherwise. Programme blocks include online Collective Vocabulary Sessions (Block 1), a 3-day in-person design sprint in Berlin (Block 2, October 2026), online implementation and festival prep (Block 3, November-December 2026), a workshop/panel presentation at transmediale (Block 4, January 2027), and ongoing long-term organising afterwards (Block 5). WHO: early-career and emerging artists, researchers, curators, students and practitioners from any discipline (art and culture, design, tech, writing, publishing, academia, law, labour organising, etc.); transdisciplinary cohorts are sought. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be based in (and around) the EU. Only one application per candidate across all labs; no collective applications. APPLY via the transmediale submission form by 7 June 2026 (23:59 CEST); participants announced 29 June 2026. Contact: submit[at]transmediale.de.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rural Creator Fellowship 2026 (News Creator Corps)
item.description:
Organisation: News Creator Corps

Location: Virtual (for rural communities in the United States)

Award: $3,500 stipend per fellow. No application fee.

Duration: Short-term virtual fellowship: ~6 weeks, meeting once a week (roughly 13 July - 21 August 2026)

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Short-term virtual fellowship from News Creator Corps that trains trusted local voices in fact-checking and reliable information sharing for rural American communities affected by local news loss. Open to two kinds of people: experienced content creators who want to deepen their fact-checking skills, and trusted community members (retired teachers, civic leaders, local business owners and others) taking first steps in public information sharing. What matters is community trust and a commitment to accuracy, not a following or equipment. The curriculum covers fact-checking and reliable sourcing, public records and FOIA requests, citing research and data, interviewing and source development, audience and trust building, audio/video tools, platform strategy/SEO/algorithms, and staying safe online. The cohort meets virtually once a week for six weeks (~13 July - 21 August 2026) with hands-on assignments and peer/program-director forums. Each fellow receives a $3,500 stipend. ELIGIBILITY: people serving rural US communities (content creators or trusted community members). Applications close 7 June 2026 (midnight ET); all applicants notified by the end of June.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MATS Autumn 2026 (ML Alignment & Theory Scholars)
item.description:
Organisation: MATS (ML Alignment & Theory Scholars)

Location: Berkeley, CA, USA and London, UK (in person)

Award: $12,500 stipend plus $20,000 compute, free housing and food, covered travel and J1 visa (if needed). Option to apply for a 6-12 month funded extension. No application fee.

Duration: 10-week program, 28 September - 4 December 2026 (optional 6-12 month funded extension)

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

MATS (ML Alignment & Theory Scholars) is a research fellowship connecting fellows with mentors from organisations such as Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Redwood Research and ARC to work on reducing frontier AI risk. The Autumn 2026 cohort runs for 10 weeks in Berkeley and London (28 Sep - 4 Dec 2026), with the option to apply for a 6-12 month funded extension. Fellows receive a $12,500 stipend, $20,000 in compute, free housing and meals, covered travel and J1 visa support, research management, and a community of peers. SEVEN TRACKS: Empirical, Theory, Strategy & Forecasting, Policy & Governance, Systems Security, Founding & Field-Building (new), and Biosecurity (new); applicants may apply to one or more. PROCESS: Stage 1 general application (1-2 hrs, due June 7); Stage 2 central evaluations (early-late June, e.g. technical/research-taste assessments for Empirical, a writing/analytical assessment for Policy & Governance); Stage 3 stream applications with stream-specific questions and possible interviews/work tests (June-late July); offers late July/early August via a two-sided matching process. ELIGIBILITY: open to researchers and field-builders internationally (travel and J1 visa covered). Applications close 7 June 2026 (EOD AOE).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Future Observatory: Prototype Grants 2026 (Sustainable Supply Chains)
item.description:
Organisation: Future Observatory and AHRC

Location: United Kingdom

Award: Up to GBP 70,000 per grant (four grants available; award range GBP 60,000-70,000). Total fund GBP 300,000. No application fee.

Duration: Prototype development and second-stage design research

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Four grants of up to GBP 70,000 from Future Observatory (the Design Museum's national design research programme) and the AHRC, awarded to UK architecture and design practices for prototype development and second-stage design research in more sustainable supply chains. Open to small-to-medium sized practices across all architecture and design disciplines, including fashion, product design, material research, systems thinking and interdisciplinary practices. Proposals should focus on sustainable supply chains, with particular interest in bioregional, biomaterial and regenerative approaches; relevant themes include retrofit, regenerative materials and systems, circular design, waste reduction and reuse, low-carbon housing, agricultural byproducts, and data-led tools and approaches. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based small-to-medium architecture and design practices. Opens 6 May 2026, 10:00am; closes 12 June 2026, 4:00pm.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Knight-Georgetown Institute: Policy Analyst 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Knight-Georgetown Institute (KGI), Georgetown University

Location: Washington, D.C., United States (hybrid; applicants must be based in D.C.)

Award: Paid role: annual salary $60,000-$70,000. No application fee.

Duration: Full-time position

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

The Knight-Georgetown Institute (KGI), Georgetown's technology-policy institute, is hiring a Policy Analyst to support its work translating research into technology and digital-governance policy. Paid role with an annual salary of $60,000-$70,000. Hybrid, but applicants must be based in Washington, D.C.; the posting does not state a citizenship or work-authorization requirement. A companion Research Analyst role is also open on the same timeline (verify on the KGI jobs page). NOTE: this is a job rather than a grant or fellowship. Applications are requested by June 1, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Talos Network: AI Policy Leaders Programme 2026 (Autumn cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Talos Network

Location: Europe (virtual bootcamp + week-long Brussels summit; placements in Brussels or other European cities)

Award: Placement track: a 12-month paid placement at an AI-focused organisation, base EUR 5,000/month for Brussels (adjusted for cost of living elsewhere). Plus a fully-reimbursed 6-day Brussels policymaking summit, tailored financial support (coworking, conference attendance, career-transition funding), and intensive 1-1 mentoring. No application fee.

Duration: Virtual bootcamp 10 Sep - 22 Oct 2026 (approx. 10 hrs/week) plus a 12-month paid placement (placement track)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

The Talos Network's Policy Leaders Programme is built for experienced professionals ready to move into shaping European governance of advanced AI. It runs a part-time virtual European AI Policy Bootcamp (10 Sep - 22 Oct 2026, weekly Thursday calls, ~10 hrs/week, designed around a full-time job) with a fully-reimbursed week-long policymaking summit in Brussels (3-9 Oct 2026), and a Placement track offering a 12-month paid placement at a relevant AI-focused organisation (base EUR 5,000/month for Brussels). Fellows get intensive 1-1 mentoring and career coaching, access to the Talos Network and alumni, a curated senior peer cohort, and tailored financial support. ELIGIBILITY: experienced professionals (around 8-10+ years) in high-stakes environments such as government, international organisations, think tanks, industry or academia, with a track record of influencing senior decision-makers, looking to transition into AI governance or strengthen the AI dimension of their existing work; Europe-focused. Application deadline 14 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Realities in Transition: RiT 2 Open Call #2 - Telepresence in the Digital Space
item.description:
Organisation: Realities in Transition, in collaboration with MEET Digital Culture Center Milan

Location: Hybrid: online phases plus a two-month in-person period at MEET Digital Culture Center, Milan, Italy

Award: Three grants of EUR 15,000 each (one per artist), covering the artist's fee plus travel, accommodation and subsistence, disbursed in three installments (EUR 7,000 on signing, EUR 4,000 after the Test Lab, EUR 4,000 at the end). Production costs of up to EUR 5,000 are covered by the host, plus a mentoring programme. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month hybrid residency (online October-December 2026; in-person at MEET Milan January-February 2027; online February-March 2027), final project presented June 2027

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Open call from the Realities in Transition (RiT 2 - Unwritten Worlds) programme for artists and designers to co-create a collective XR artwork during a residency exploring telepresence and the shifting nature of presence in the digital age. The residency investigates how the body operates across physical and digital dimensions through motion capture and immersive environments, treating movement as a language that can be encoded, transformed and transmitted, and opening critical and speculative perspectives on authorship, identity and corporeality. MEDIUM: motion capture and immersivity. ELIGIBILITY: individual XR and immersive artists who are European residents; three artists are selected and joined by a creative technologist to work as a group. The residency alternates shared work, critical discussion and experimentation, from conception and prototyping to a public Test Lab and audience presentation, with a mentoring programme covering XR mediation, ethics and inclusion, accessibility, hybridisation of spaces, distribution and business models. Apply via the Realities in Transition open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America Literary Grants 2027
item.description:
Organisation: PEN America

Location: United States (US-based writers)

Award: Varies by grant (e.g. PEN/Jean Stein Grant for Literary Oral History, PEN/Phyllis Naylor Working Writer Fellowship), generally in the $5,000 to $10,000+ range. No application fee.

Duration: Project / works-in-progress support

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's suite of literary grants and fellowships supporting individual writers and works in progress across genres (fiction, nonfiction, poetry, oral history and more), each with its own focus and criteria. ELIGIBILITY: varies by grant; generally US-based writers, with several open to writers and translators at different career stages. Distinct from the PEN/Heim Translation Fund and PEN Presents. Review individual grant criteria and apply via the PEN America literary grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: David McClure Public Interest Journalism Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Oxford International Centre for Publishing, Oxford Brookes University

Location: Fully remote

Award: GBP 15,000 grant to support one nonfiction writing fellow. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship starts each September (funding for one fellow per year for five years)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Fellowship from the Oxford International Centre for Publishing at Oxford Brookes University, supporting one nonfiction writer per year (for the next five years) with a GBP 15,000 award to work on a timely, independent piece of long-form public interest journalism that might otherwise never be published. The investigation must be original, non-biased, non-partisan and rigorously researched, with the decision based on how deserving the work is to be in the public domain. Named for David McClure, the news producer, author and journalist known for his forensic investigations into institutional privilege and the British Royal family's finances. APPLICATION: a 1,500-word pitch including a synopsis of the investigation and its aims, objectives, key research undertaken and progress to date, the support needed to complete it, and how the funds will be used. The fellow starts in September; the annual application deadline is 31 May. Fully remote.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here Residency 2027 (One-Year and One-Month)
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland

Award: Funded. One-Year residency: EUR 1,000/month bursary for contracted months, free studio and accommodation, plus one return journey for residents from outside Ireland. One-Month residency: EUR 250/week stipend, free private bedroom and shared studio, plus a return journey for international participants. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee (Submittable).

Duration: One-year or one-month residency during 2027

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here residency programme offers research-led residencies in Dublin across the visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields, open to artists and practitioners based in Ireland or internationally. One application covers two formats: a One-Year residency (monthly EUR 1,000 bursary, free studio and accommodation, travel for non-Ireland residents) and a One-Month residency (EUR 250/week stipend, free room and shared studio, travel for international participants). Suited to research, critical-writing and cross-disciplinary practice including new-media work. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally; framed around research excellence rather than career stage. Deadline Monday 22 June 2026, 14:00 GMT; a EUR 10 application fee applies via Submittable. See the IMMA open-calls page for the separate one-year and one-month application links.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Bayt AlMamzar x Scanteia: Bucharest Residency Exchange 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Bayt AlMamzar (BAM) and Scanteia

Location: Residency in Bucharest, Romania (applicants must be UAE-based)

Award: Funded residency: private ~50 sqm studio with accommodation at Atelierele Scanteia, flight covered up to $500, $500 artist fee, local transport card, access to large-format inkjet printing, photo studio, wood workshop and scanners, plus a curated joint exhibition. No application fee.

Duration: One month (Residency 1: Aug-Sep 2026; Residency 2: mid-March to mid-April 2027), with a joint exhibition end of April 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

An international exchange between Bayt AlMamzar (BAM), an artist-run space in Dubai, and Scanteia, an artist-run space in Bucharest, inviting UAE-based artists to undertake individual month-long residencies in Bucharest across 2026-2027, followed by a joint exhibition curated by Scanteia. Two artists are selected for non-overlapping residencies (Aug-Sep 2026 and mid-March to mid-April 2027). Scanteia provides a private ~50 sqm studio with live-in accommodation, a flight subsidy up to $500, a $500 artist fee, a local transport card, weekly studio visits with Romanian curators and gallerists, study trips (MARe, MNAC, EVA Foundation), access to specialised equipment (large-scale inkjet printing, photography studio, wood workshop, scanners), exhibition space, and documentation/publication support via BAM's publishing arm BAMBAM. ELIGIBILITY: open to visual artists, researchers and practitioners currently based in the UAE, aged 18+, at any career stage; a valid UAE residence visa is required at the time of application. No nationality restriction, but applicants without an EU or Emirati passport will need a Schengen visa (Scanteia provides an invitation letter). They seek artists with a developed practice and a specific, articulable reason to spend time in Bucharest (exploration, research, production or exchange), with no fixed theme or medium. Deadline 7 June 2026. Contact hello@baytalmazmar.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FPP Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Konstkollektivet / Flower Power Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Konstkollektivet, with Flower Power Photography, Centrum for fotografi and Artist in Coexistence (AiC)

Location: Orust and Molndal, Sweden

Award: Artist fee 7,000 SEK plus exhibition fee 3,400 SEK, travel support up to 4,000 SEK (slow travel by train/bus encouraged), and accommodation for the whole period. Plus facilitation, mentorship and research support. No application fee. (Amounts in SEK, incl. VAT where applicable.)

Duration: 14-day residency 3-17 August 2026; group exhibition opens 1 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Residency and exhibition for visual artists whose practice engages with experimental, alternative and/or critical image-making (analogue processes, archives or hybrid approaches), with a particular interest in image-making as a way of living and thinking alongside the planet and other beings, embracing slowness, collaboration and more-than-human care. Organised by Konstkollektivet with Flower Power Photography (an artistic research project by Sam McCarthy and Lasse Lindqvist on non-violence, ecology and more-than-human ethics), Centrum for fotografi, and Artist in Coexistence (AiC). Includes a 14-day residency on the island of Orust (3-15 August), a process presentation, a workshop contribution at Konstkollektivet (15-16 August), and participation in a group exhibition at Konstparken opening 1 September, plus accommodation, an artist fee (7,000 SEK), exhibition fee (3,400 SEK), and travel support up to 4,000 SEK. ELIGIBILITY: European-based visual artists of all ages and nationalities. APPLICATION: one PDF (max 10MB) with a project proposal/motivation (max 1 page), artist statement (max 150 words), CV (1 page) and portfolio (max 5 pages) to residens@konstkollektivet.se, subject line 'Name_LastName_FPP_Residency', by 14 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prairie Ronde Artist Residency: Summer/Fall 2026 (Michigan)
item.description:
Organisation: The Mill at Vicksburg

Location: Vicksburg, Michigan, USA (5-6 week on-site residency, private housing)

Award: $2,000 stipend plus a $500 travel grant and private housing for the 5-6 week residency. Application fee: $25.

Duration: Five- to six-week residency

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026

Prairie Ronde Artist Residency at The Mill at Vicksburg, a self-directed residency open to artists across disciplines including film, video and new media. ELIGIBILITY: artists of any discipline; open worldwide. Apply via the Prairie Ronde application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lux Capital Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Lux Capital

Location: United States (in-person retreats; remote project work)

Award: $15,000 non-dilutive grant (no strings attached, no need to incorporate a company), plus mentorship and all-expenses-paid retreats. No application fee.

Duration: Summer fellowship (kick-off late July; closes late August/early September 2026)

Deadline: 13 Jun 2026

Fellowship from venture-capital firm Lux Capital honouring exceptional undergraduates at the intersection of science and technology, pushing the boundaries of the physical, computational and life sciences. No prior experience required; flexible by design to run alongside other academic and professional commitments. FOCUS AREAS: Physical Sciences (next-gen materials, defense, chips, energy, space); Life Sciences (healthcare, computational biology, new therapeutics); and Computational Sciences (AI, cryptography, open source, infrastructure, dev tools). WHO THEY SEEK: scrappy, high-velocity builders who ship fast; deeply technical engineering and scientific pioneers (startup designation does not matter); and seekers with something to prove. BENEFITS: a $15,000 non-dilutive grant to offset project costs (no strings attached, no need to formally incorporate); a personalized mentor from the Lux portfolio and investment team; all-expenses-paid retreats at the start and end of the summer; and sessions ranging from fireside chats with portfolio founders to themed discussions. ELIGIBILITY: undergraduates (team applications accepted). TIMELINE: applications due June 13, 2026; results late June; fellowship kick-off late July; close late August/early September. Contact: flux@luxcapital.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Alan Andres Writing Fellowships 2026-2027 (Boston Public Library)
item.description:
Organisation: Associates of the Boston Public Library

Location: Boston / New England, United States

Award: Two fellowships (apply to only one): Writer-in-Residence: $70,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support and a year-long office residency at the Boston Public Library. Picture Book Writer Fellowship: $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support. No application fee.

Duration: Writer-in-Residence: one year (October 2026 to September 2027)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

The Associates of the Boston Public Library offer the Alan Andres Writing Fellowships for emerging New England writers, funded by an anonymous donor committed to writers earning a living wage. Applicants may apply to only one fellowship; winners are chosen through a blind judging process. (1) ALAN ANDRES WRITER-IN-RESIDENCE (22nd year): a year-long residency (October 2026 to September 2027) with an office at the Boston Public Library, a $70,000 stipend, and $2,500 for editorial support, to complete a manuscript for children or young adults (fiction, nonfiction, plays, poetry, or graphic novels); apply at www.writer-in-residence.org. (2) ALAN ANDRES PICTURE BOOK WRITER FELLOWSHIP (formerly the Illustrated Children's Book Fellowship, 2nd year): a $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 for editorial support to complete a manuscript for young readers (fiction, nonfiction, memoir, or poetry for ages 0-2 board books or ages 3-8 picture books and early readers); apply at www.associatesbpl.org/picturebook. ELIGIBILITY: emerging New England writers. Applications close June 5, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Library: Creative Fellowship 2026 - Remote Ecologies (Art, Climate and the Amazon)
item.description:
Organisation: The British Library

Location: London / UK-based

Award: GBP 7,500 stipend plus up to GBP 3,000 production budget.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

A collection-based artistic research fellowship at the British Library responding to climate and the Amazon through the Library's holdings. The fellow develops new work informed by the collections over roughly a year (September 2026 to July 2027), receiving a GBP 7,500 stipend and up to GBP 3,000 for production. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based artists, over 18, not currently a student, with a public track record and an interest in collection-based research and climate issues.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Trust: Grants for Artist Projects (GAP) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Artist Trust

Location: Washington State, United States

Award: $2,500 unrestricted project grant per artist (awarded to 65 artists). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

Unrestricted project-based grants of $2,500 from Artist Trust to 65 Washington State artists across all disciplines, including literary and media arts. Funds can support a specific project or career-advancing activity. ELIGIBILITY: Washington State residents who are originators of works of art and not current students; open to artist teams.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Agog: Climate Futures Open Call 2026 (Immersive Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Agog: Impact in Immersive Media

Location: Remote / project-based (US-registered entity or fiscal sponsor required; international collaborators allowed)

Award: $25,000 to $200,000 per grant (up to $1M total pool). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Open call from Agog for immersive-media projects that drive climate engagement and action, using augmented and mixed reality, spatial sound, smart glasses and related technologies. ELIGIBILITY: creators, artists and teams working in immersive media; applicants must apply through a US-registered entity or fiscal sponsor, though international collaborators are allowed. Apply via the Agog open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kate Medina Fellowship for Literary Narrative Nonfiction 2026 (NYPL)
item.description:
Organisation: New York Public Library, in partnership with Random House

Location: New York City, USA (on-site research at the NYPL Schwarzman Building)

Award: $30,000 stipend for four continuous months of research (September 2026 to March 2027). No application fee.

Duration: Four continuous months of research

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Fellowship from the New York Public Library and Random House for writers of literary narrative nonfiction whose projects engage NYPL's archival and special collections. ELIGIBILITY: writers of narrative nonfiction with a project requiring on-site access to the collections at the Stephen A. Schwarzman Building in New York. Apply via the NYPL fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pulitzer Center: U.S. Civil Society Microgrants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Pulitzer Center

Location: United States

Award: US$2,000 to US$4,000 (draft budget required)

Duration: Up to six months (late June to early December 2026); project closure and impact reporting by December 21, 2026

Deadline: 08 Jun 2026

Microgrant supporting U.S. civil society organizations and groups to build civic engagement projects that use Pulitzer Center-supported reporting to amplify the voices of affected communities, foster public debate, and drive meaningful audience engagement. Projects must engage at least one Pulitzer Center story and/or journalist and focus on one of three issue areas: Information and Artificial Intelligence (impact of AI on society, AI governance dialogue); Climate and the Environment (U.S. fisheries and fishing communities, or climate and labor); or Global Health (mental well-being, or connections to Global South reporting). Supported activities include multi-stakeholder dialogues, public forums, community engagement, creative campaigns (videos, infographics), and other innovative formats such as art exhibitions. Out of scope: projects not using Pulitzer Center reporting, direct advocacy or lobbying, partisan campaigns, and for-profit initiatives. ELIGIBILITY: grassroots organizations, academics and researchers, education institutions, civil society organizations and coalitions, youth movements, and other groups with past experience implementing activities in the United States, a track record of collaboration, and proven experience on the identified topics. APPLICATION: project description (max 400 words), target audience and projected reach, methodology, implementation timeline, preliminary budget, content production and distribution plan if applicable, lead applicant CV plus a letter of recommendation. Selection involves shortlisting and an interview with the lead applicant. A required onboarding meeting for grantees is Wednesday, June 24, 2026 (7:00-8:30pm EDT).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lanesboro Arts: Early Career Artist Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Lanesboro Arts

Location: Lanesboro, Minnesota, United States (pop. 754)

Award: $1,250 weekly stipend (fixed per residency, including for small collaborating teams); housing provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two or four week residency, scheduled within February 1 to December 31, 2027

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Residency program in the rural community of Lanesboro, MN, supported by the Jerome Foundation, offering early career artists two or four week residencies to explore how their work can engage the community and how a rural community can inform their work. Staff co-create a fully customized residency experience around community engagement. Lanesboro Arts offers two to three residencies per year; the program provides an individual studio plus access to conference rooms, desk space, a gallery, piano and stage, with private housing (apartment, kitchen, private room and bathroom). No meals provided. Open to all disciplines. ELIGIBILITY: legal residents of Minnesota or one of the five boroughs of New York City for at least one year prior to applying; defined as early career artists with at least a 2-year and no more than 10-year track record of creating and presenting their own projects (not first-time creators or beginning artists). Degree-seeking students at the time of application or during the grant period (K-12, college, graduate or postgraduate) are not eligible. Age is not a factor. The primary goal must be to generate new work rather than remount or re-interpret existing work. Apply via the online webform on the Lanesboro Arts website. Selection announced June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eloquence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney (Culture+)

Location: Australia (participating universities)

Award: National public speaking prize (award for the winning art talk on French and Australian cultural ties). No application fee.

Duration: Three-stage competition during 2026 (university shortlist, recorded talk, national live final)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual national public speaking challenge for students at participating Australian universities, rewarding the best art talk on French and Australian cultural ties. The 2026 theme is 'Art and Environment, France and Australia: Our forests and trees in danger': students compare two artists, one Australian and one French (at least one a visual artist), analysing how a sensibility to nature shaped their work on forests and trees from the 19th century to the present. THREE STAGES: (1) submit a 400-word summary paper outlining a proposed 7-minute talk; university panels shortlist three applicants; (2) shortlisted applicants submit a recording of the full 7-minute talk and panels select one representative per university; (3) representatives compete in a live (or recorded) national final. Stage 1 summaries are judged on compliance with instructions (10%), quality of sources (15%), relevance to theme (15%), writing and structure (20%), and interest/depth/originality (40%). ELIGIBILITY: students in Arts or Humanities disciplines; in the final year of a Bachelor degree, or Honours, Graduate Certificate, Masters or PhD students; enrolled for the first semester of the year at a participating institution; over 18. Stage 1 due 11:59PM, 31 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Blackstone Publishing Novel Initiative 2026 (with The Black List)
item.description:
Organisation: The Black List and Blackstone Publishing

Location: Online (manuscripts hosted on blcklst.com)

Award: A $25,000 publishing deal with Blackstone Publishing for the selected manuscript.

Duration: Submission period November 20, 2025 to June 9, 2026

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

A partnership between The Black List and Blackstone Publishing to identify an unpublished manuscript for a $25,000 publishing deal. The Black List helps Blackstone shortlist outstanding manuscripts submitted on blcklst.com during the submission period. Open to manuscripts in all genres, with particular interest in work that elevates familiar themes (love, adventure, guilt, desire, betrayal, family, identity, obsession) from fresh, compelling perspectives, especially thrillers, high-concept mysteries, romances and horror. REQUIREMENTS: post an original manuscript on blcklst.com, receive at least one evaluation while hosted there (once evaluated, a manuscript can be submitted to any eligible partnership at no additional cost), and opt in to the program during the submission period. Submitted manuscripts must not be under contract elsewhere; no feature, pilot, play or musical submissions are eligible. Applicants must be at least 18 and the sole owner of all rights to wholly original material, and must agree to the Submission Agreement (which includes legally binding terms such as arbitration). If selected, the writer may enter into a book deal negotiated in good faith. Writing teams are allowed, but every member must comply and all must opt out if any member becomes ineligible. Note: submitting requires being logged in to blcklst.com with an uploaded project (the site charges separately for hosting and evaluations). Deadline June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BRIClab: Film + TV Residency 2026/27 (Brooklyn)
item.description:
Organisation: BRIC

Location: Brooklyn, New York, USA (NYC-based)

Award: $2,500 stipend plus up to $2,000 in production costs, alongside equipment and post-production access and mentorship. No application fee.

Duration: Residency across 2026/27

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

BRIClab Film + TV residency at BRIC in Brooklyn, supporting emerging-to-mid-career documentary filmmakers to develop a project with a stipend, production funds, equipment and post-production access, and mentorship. ELIGIBILITY: NYC-based documentary filmmakers, individuals or duos; the programme prioritises disabled, BIPOC and LGBTQ+ artists. Apply via the BRIC opportunity page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Film London: The FLAMIN Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Film London (FLAMIN - Film London Artists' Moving Image Network), with Arts Council England

Location: England, UK (year-long programme with in-person workshops)

Award: GBP 2,500 project-development bursary per artist, plus a year-long programme of monthly in-person workshops, 1:1 sessions with a production advisor, screening and promotion, and a peer community. Stage-2 interviewees also receive a GBP 100 interview fee. No application fee.

Duration: One-year fellowship programme

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

The FLAMIN Fellowship from Film London supports early-career artists working in moving image with a development bursary and a structured year-long career-development programme. ELIGIBILITY: England-based moving-image artists with roughly 2 to 5 years of professional practice outside higher education and at least two prior exhibited or screened works; excludes current students, gallery-represented artists, those with major solo institutional shows, and film-industry professionals. Apply via the Film London FLAMIN Fellowship page by midnight on 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artists' Futures Fund: Breakthrough Artist Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Artists' Futures Fund

Location: England and Wales, UK (in-kind studio space at a partner institution)

Award: GBP 10,000 unrestricted bursary over the 10-month fellowship, plus in-kind studio space, mentorship from a partner institution, professional development, and a co-curated end-of-fellowship group showcase. No application fee.

Duration: 10 months (October 2026 to July 2027)

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Needs-based fellowship from the Artists' Futures Fund bridging the gap from graduation to professional visual-arts practice. ELIGIBILITY: recent BA or MA visual-arts graduates (within two years of October 2026) from an AFF partner institution (Cardiff School of Art & Design, Chichester College Group, Liverpool Hope, Loughborough, Manchester Metropolitan, City of Portsmouth College, Sunderland, Swansea College of Art UWTSD), or a relevant Level 3 UAL Diploma/A Levels from City of Portsmouth College; must be eligible to live and work in England or Wales, facing socio-economic, mental and/or physical health barriers, and able to commit fully to the 10 months without additional formal study. Applications open 15 May 2026. Apply via the Artists' Futures Fund support page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence 2026 (Oak Spring)
item.description:
Organisation: Oak Spring Garden Foundation

Location: Upperville, Virginia, USA (2-5 week on-site residency)

Award: $10,000 grant plus a two- to five-week residency with accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two- to five-week residency

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence from the Oak Spring Garden Foundation, supporting early-career artists whose work engages plants, gardens and landscapes. ELIGIBILITY: early-career visual and literary artists, dancers and musicians; no nationality restriction stated, though the residency takes place on site in Virginia. Apply via the Oak Spring fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN Presents x SALT: Sample Translation Grants 2026 (South Asian languages)
item.description:
Organisation: English PEN, in partnership with SALT

Location: Remote (worldwide translators; source works in South Asian languages)

Award: GBP 500 to each of 24 shortlisted translators for a 5,000-word sample translation; 12 winners additionally receive editorial support and promotion. No application fee.

Duration: One-off sample-translation commission

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Special PEN Presents round in partnership with SALT, funding sample translations from South Asian languages to help unpublished works find English-language publishers. The scheme pays for the sample-translation work that is usually unpaid. ELIGIBILITY: individual literary translators at any career stage, working from source languages of Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, the Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan or Sri Lanka. Apply via the English PEN PEN Presents page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Diriyah Art Futures: Emerging New Media Artists Program 2026/27 (Riyadh)
item.description:
Organisation: Diriyah Art Futures (DAF), in collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains

Location: Diriyah, Saudi Arabia (in-person, intensive on-site programme)

Award: Production budget; access to advanced audio-visual labs, immersive-reality and spatial audio-visual environments; cutting-edge professional equipment; multidisciplinary mentorship from international digital and new-media artists; and a culminating curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. Specific stipend amount not stated in the public call. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately one year (programme starts October 2026), with three months of thematic and conceptual seminars followed by a practice-based phase and a final group exhibition in October 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Third cycle of Diriyah Art Futures' Emerging New Media Artists Program, a developmental collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains. The first three months centre on thematic and conceptual exploration through seminars, lectures, screenings and workshops spanning digital technology, socio-cultural dynamics, the environment, more-than-human life and cultural heritage. The programme then moves into a practice-based phase with personalised advisory and access to DAF's labs, encompassing advanced audio-visual techniques, immersive reality and spatial audio-visual environments, concluding with a curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. ELIGIBILITY: emerging creative practitioners under 35, at graduate or postgraduate level, with experience in modern media and digital art; open worldwide with a particular focus on practitioners from the Middle East and North Africa. Apply via the linked Ministry of Culture engagement portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CBPS Research Fellowship 2026-27 (Bengaluru)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Budget and Policy Studies (CBPS)

Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India (in-person at CBPS office)

Award: INR 25,000 per month stipend, plus access to office space, internet and the CBPS library. The fellowship does NOT cover housing, transportation or any other financial support. No application fee.

Duration: One year (1 August 2026 to 31 July 2027)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

One-year research fellowship at the Centre for Budget and Policy Studies (CBPS), a non-partisan public policy research institution in Bengaluru, mentoring early-career researchers across CBPS's focus areas: education, gender, governance, health and nutrition, public finance, livelihoods and social security. Fellows work alongside CBPS research staff in a collegial academic environment with in-house talks, seminars, workshops and public events, joining ongoing research projects with mentor guidance. ELIGIBILITY: primarily geared toward scholars who have recently completed their Bachelor's or are in the final year of an undergraduate programme; applications especially encouraged from historically underrepresented groups (class, caste, gender, religion, linguistic affiliation, first-generation scholars); scholars from various disciplines and methodological approaches welcome. Apply by emailing a Statement of Purpose (max 1,500 words), CV/resume in MS Word format, and two letters of reference to fellows@cbps.in. Fellowship awards announced 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Grove and Pines Film Festival: Erin Carine Grant for Queer Creatives 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Grove and Pines Film Festival (GAPFF)

Location: United States (festival is US-based; geographic eligibility not specified on the grant page)

Award: $5,000 USD grant awarded annually at the festival. No application fee mentioned.

Duration: One-off project grant

Deadline: 10 Jun 2026

Annual grant from the Grove and Pines Film Festival honouring the legacy of Erin Carine, awarded to a filmmaker whose project embodies creativity, courage and a deep commitment to authentic queer storytelling. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual filmmakers; applicants do not need to have a finished film. The public grant page does not state geographic restrictions, age limits or career-stage requirements - confirm on the linked page before applying. Apply via the Google Form linked from the festival's grant-info page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS): International Creator Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS), Tokyo Metropolitan Foundation for History and Culture

Location: TOKAS Residency, 2-14-7 Tatekawa, Sumida-ku, Tokyo, Japan (single room 25 sqm; twin room 49 sqm for duos; shared studios 125 sqm and 48 sqm)

Award: Round-trip economy-class airfare between nearest airport and Tokyo (Narita or Haneda); per diem living expenses of JPY 4,200 per day (excl. tax); fee for work/project of JPY 300,000 (excl. tax); living space (single room) and access to shared studio. Note: 20.42% income tax is deducted from per-diem and project-fee amounts. For duos, airfare is provided for both members but per diem and project fee are paid for one person only. No application fee.

Duration: Three-month residency in one of three slots: May-July 2027, September-November 2027, or January-March 2028 (six creators total, two per slot), concluding with an Open Studio research presentation

Deadline: 16 Jun 2026

Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS) International Creator Residency invites practitioners to develop new works and ideas in Tokyo, with research outcomes presented at a closing Open Studio at TOKAS Residency. The programme fosters cross-cultural creative exchange across visual arts (drawing, installation, painting, performance, photography, media art, sculpture, sound art, video, etc.), design and architecture. ELIGIBILITY: creators residing outside Japan with at least five years of experience in visual arts, design or architecture; sufficient English to communicate with residents and TOKAS staff; independent and capable of working and living on one's own; duos eligible if at least one member resides outside Japan and they have completed at least one prior project together; students ineligible except PhD candidates. CONDITIONS: must stay consecutively at TOKAS Residency for the proposed period; create work for the Open Studio and submit a comprehensive project report; cooperate with TOKAS public relations; no commercial or for-profit activities; accommodation is for the participating creator only (no family/partners/friends/guests). Apply via the TOKAS online submission form by 16 June 2026, 18:00 JST: application form (PDF, in English), two letters of recommendation from referees at art and cultural institutions, and a portfolio (max 6 A4 landscape pages, max 10MB, up to 3 projects).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BC Arts Council: Individual Arts Grants - Media Artists 2026
item.description:
Organisation: BC Arts Council (Province of British Columbia)

Location: British Columbia, Canada (provincial council; BC residency typically required)

Award: Up to $25,000 CAD per project. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 17 Jun 2026

BC Arts Council's Individual Arts Grants stream for independent media artists working in moving image (film, video, narrative, experimental, expanded cinema, installation), audio/sound art (sound sculptures, installations, sound walks, gallery presentations) or new media and digital arts (interactive installations, immersive environments, web-based art, ICT). ELIGIBILITY: independent individual media artists (not groups or organisations); projects must be independent of commercial industries. As a provincial arts council, BC Arts Council typically requires applicants to be residents of British Columbia - confirm current residency requirements on the linked page before applying. NOTE: registration must be completed by 17 June 2026 at 23:59 PT, ahead of the 24 June application deadline. Applications via the SmartSimple Grant Management System. Contact: Paneet Singh, Program Advisor (Paneet.Singh@gov.bc.ca).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Berggruen Institute: Peking University Research Center Fellowship 2026-2027 (Beijing)
item.description:
Organisation: Berggruen Institute China Center, Peking University

Location: Beijing, China (on-site engagement at PKU expected)

Award: 200,000 CNY annual stipend, paid monthly before tax. Fellows are responsible for their own income tax. No application fee.

Duration: One year, part-time, running 2026 to 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Part-time research fellowship at the Berggruen Institute's Research Center at Peking University, working within one of three core themes (Frontier Science, Technology and Philosophy; Creative Futures; Ancient Wisdom and Planetary Governance), with strong overlap for AI policy, philosophy of technology and computational practice. ELIGIBILITY: open to applicants from any research background; selection is based on the proposal's fit with the Center's themes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Goethe-Institut: Individual Mobility Grants 2026 (Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe)
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: Mobility between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe

Award: Travel costs up to EUR 1,800 (from Europe), accommodation, daily allowances and project expenses covered. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based mobility (variable length)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Goethe-Institut individual mobility grants for artists, researchers and curators (including digital and computational practitioners) for research, collaboration, residency placements or project development between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists, researchers and cultural workers. QUARTERLY DEADLINES: 15 June 2026, 15 September 2026 and 15 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature / Toji Cultural Centre Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature and Toji Cultural Foundation

Location: Toji Cultural Centre, Wonju, South Korea (eligibility restricted to writers nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature)

Award: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, meals and local transportation are all covered. NO artist fee or honorarium.

Duration: Eight weeks, 1 September - 31 October 2026

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eight-week fully funded residency at the Toji Cultural Centre in Wonju, South Korea, hosted by Wonju UNESCO City of Literature. ELIGIBILITY: one writer per cycle, nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature network (Amsterdam, Utrecht, Manchester, Edinburgh, Krakow, Reykjavik, Lillehammer, Iowa City, etc. - see the full UNESCO Creative Cities Network list); 5+ years writing experience required. Open to fiction, nonfiction and poetry. PROCESS: selection by 21 July 2026; residency runs 1 September - 31 October 2026. Apply through your local UNESCO City of Literature coordinator before 31 May 2026. Confirm the exact application route on the Wonju City of Literature site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: JSK Journalism Fellowship 2027 (John S. Knight Fellowships at Stanford)
item.description:
Organisation: John S. Knight Journalism Fellowships at Stanford

Location: Stanford, California, USA (residential 9-month fellowship)

Award: USD 130,000 stipend over 9 months, plus Stanford tuition, housing, health insurance, and a dependent supplement. No application fee.

Duration: 9 months at Stanford (academic year)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Stanford's flagship journalism fellowship, funded by the John S. Knight Foundation. A nine-month residential, project-based fellowship for working journalists to reimagine the future of journalism - many JSK fellows tackle AI, platforms, information ecosystems, accountability or audience trust. ELIGIBILITY: working journalists with at least 5 years of professional experience; full-time staff, freelancers and entrepreneurial journalists are all eligible; international applicants welcome. PROCESS: application portal opened 6 May 2026; deadline 2 June 2026 at 1pm Pacific. Distinct from the Knight Cities Challenge on the desk - both are Knight-funded but JSK is the Stanford residential fellowship for individual journalists. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Taiwan AID: 2026 NGO Fellowship Program (Asia and Pacific Islands)
item.description:
Organisation: Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan (fully on-site participation required, including a three-week field placement at a Taiwanese NGO)

Award: Fully funded: round-trip airfare, accommodation (dormitory-style rooms, 6-8 beds per room), meals, and local transportation in Taiwan are all covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Four weeks, 4 October - 1 November 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID) annual fellowship for NGO professionals from South Asia, Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. Approximately 12 fellows selected (tentative) for a four-week immersive programme combining (a) a three-week placement at a Taiwanese NGO (event preparation, project management, fundraising activities), (b) workshops on project management, fundraising and advocacy plus civil society site visits in Taiwan, (c) a roundtable with Taiwanese NGO professionals, and (d) attendance at Taiwan LGBT+ Pride. Fellows propose a project draft for future collaboration with their host NGO at the end of the programme and join an alumni network across Asia. ELIGIBILITY: NGO professionals (community development, environment, education, women's rights, civic engagement and human rights, emergency relief, disability inclusion, children and families welfare, social innovation and entrepreneurship; outstanding applicants from other fields also considered); at least three years of NGO sector experience; aged 25-40; from Southeast Asia (Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Timor-Leste, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam), South Asia (Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Maldives, Pakistan), Northeast Asia (Japan, South Korea, Mongolia) or Pacific Islands (Palau, Marshall Islands, Tuvalu); proficient in written and oral English (Mandarin a plus).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Flaherty: 2026 Fellowship (71st Flaherty Film Seminar)
item.description:
Organisation: The Flaherty

Location: Vermont, USA (in-person fellows) OR remote (online fellows)

Award: Complimentary Seminar registration for both online and in-person tracks. In-person fellows additionally receive lodging and meals for the duration of the Seminar. The Fellowship does NOT cover travel to and from Vermont. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Programme runs 7-10 October 2026 (in-person fellows arrive 7 October, depart 11 October); pre-Seminar onboarding in early September; written feedback report due 26 October 2026

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

The Flaherty's 2026 Fellowship programme for the 71st Flaherty Film Seminar, welcoming 15 in-person fellows in Vermont and 15 online fellows. Of these, 8 in-person and 9 online slots are filled through this open call (the remaining slots are underwritten by academic institutions who select recipients internally). Fellows participate in the full Seminar programme, share their work in Fellows' Screenings and Presentations to Seminar programmers and artists, attend meetings and discussions with featured artists and special guests, and may be asked to facilitate breakout discussions. ELIGIBILITY: active in the field through research, filmmaking or related organisational or project-based work; at least 21 years old; has not received an In-Person Flaherty Seminar Fellowship in the last five years; available for the full duration of the programme. No academic background required. The Flaherty actively encourages applications from BIPOC participants. OBLIGATIONS: pre-Seminar onboarding (virtual) in early September; full attendance through the Seminar 7-10 October 2026; written feedback report due 26 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America: PEN/Heim Translation Fund Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: PEN America

Location: Worldwide (translators of any nationality; project must translate a book-length work into English)

Award: USD 4,000 per project (up to 10 awards per year). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (book-length literary translation in progress)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's annual grants for in-progress book-length literary translations from any language into English. Up to 10 grants of USD 4,000 each. Preference for early-career translators and works from underrepresented languages and regions. Eligible genres include fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry and drama. ELIGIBILITY: translators of any nationality; the project must be a translation of a book-length work into English; previous PEN/Heim recipients are eligible after a waiting period. No application fee. Apply by 15 June 2026 via PEN America's grants portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco)
item.description:
Organisation: The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Video Pool: Video Commission Residency 2026 - VCR Vol.4 (Attrition)
item.description:
Organisation: Video Pool Media Arts Centre

Location: Remote (no in-person attendance required); final showcase at Video Pool's Poolside Gallery in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada. Equipment/facilities access only for artists based in Winnipeg.

Award: $1,500 artist fee per selected artist (currency not explicitly stated; likely CAD as Video Pool is Winnipeg-based). Artists based in Winnipeg additionally receive access to VP's facilities and equipment (valued at $1,500). Final works added to Video Pool's distribution catalogue and archive.

Duration: Residency period 6 July - 11 September 2026; exhibition September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Video Pool Media Arts Centre's 4th Video Commission Residency, VCR Vol.4 'Attrition', commissioning new experimental single-channel video works (5-20 minutes) responding to the theme of attrition: conditions shaped by sustained contact and duration, in images, materials and environments, where form and meaning change through accumulating processes. Artists are encouraged to consider how images and environments are worn down through use, working with analog or digital processes (or both) to explore feedback systems, compression, copying, translation, repeated handling and similar processes that produce difference with each iteration. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists (no nationality restriction stated). No in-person attendance required during the creation process - effectively remote-friendly.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IT for Change: FemFirst Research Observatory 2026 (Feminist AI Futures in South and Southeast Asia)
item.description:
Organisation: IT for Change

Location: South and Southeast Asia preferred; applicants located in other countries are considered if the research focuses on the region

Award: Approximately CAD 12,000 per proposal for a 12-month period

Duration: 12 months

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

The FemFirst Research Observatory supports mid- to senior-career women and non-binary-identifying researchers and scholars in contributing original research on feminist AI futures in South and Southeast Asia. The Observatory will support 8 to 12 researchers, each developing two high-quality original research papers (6,000 words each) over a 12-month period, alongside two blog syntheses and additional dissemination materials such as podcast episodes. AIMS: build a robust body of empirical, conceptual, policy and applied research at the intersection of AI and gender equality in South and Southeast Asia; open new technical, social and political pathways for feminist AI; and seed an interdisciplinary AI knowledge ecosystem and network of researchers and institutions in the region. ELIGIBILITY: established mid- to senior-career researchers and scholars; may be independent researchers, individual researchers, scholars or academics, or research teams at registered research institutions or entities (universities, research centres, non-profit organisations, for-profit firms). Applicants must demonstrate regional expertise and specialisation in South and Southeast Asia with a track record of work on digitalisation and gender. Applicants must identify as a woman or non-binary person; team applications must be led by a woman or non-binary person. Researchers located outside South or Southeast Asia are considered, but research must focus on the region. Proposed research must be original and not published elsewhere; any additional funding sources for the same proposal must be disclosed.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
